key_insight: The paper proposes a hybrid intelligent control scheme that combines soft-voting ensemble learning with PSO-optimized fuzzy control to reduce laser welding deviation in skin-frame structures.
engineering_relevance: This approach targets more stable, accurate laser welding of complex frame assemblies, which is relevant for improving manufacturing precision and reducing defect rates in aerospace and similar structural applications.
method: Soft-voting ensemble learning is used to infer welding deviation patterns.
Particle swarm optimization (PSO) tunes fuzzy controller parameters.
The controller is designed for real-time deviation regulation during laser welding.
evidence_signal: The abstract indicates a journal article in Optics & Laser Technology, but it does not provide specific dataset size, validation metrics, or quantitative performance results.
watch_item: Implementation may depend on representative training data and careful tuning for different joint geometries, materials, and shop-floor process disturbances.
paper_title: Intelligent regulation of laser welding deviation for skin-frame structures based on soft voting ensemble learning and PSO-optimized fuzzy control
